$comment: RND-5 canonical head-to-head benchmark spec — the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for both the
Canopy bench app and its byte-identical RN 0.76.9 sibling. Both apps render the SAME three
workloads with the SAME dimensions, colors and row count so the comparison is apples-to-apples.
The scripted driver (scripts/bench-compare.sh) flings/taps each side identically and captures
the SAME gfxinfo/meminfo, and scripts/compare-report.js ratifies the perf bar from this file's
`gates` block. Change a number here and BOTH apps + the gate move together — that coupling is
the whole point: a benchmark is only honest if the two sides cannot silently diverge.

viewport.comment: logical dp; the scripted fling math is fraction-of-screen so it is resolution-independent

list1000.title: 1000-row list
list1000.comment: Canopy renders this via Native.List (windowed); RN renders it via FlatList. Both are virtualized — the fair comparison is two windowing list impls, NOT a windowed list vs a 1000-view dump.

counter.title: counter (tap latency)
counter.comment: Each tap increments a single Int and re-renders ONE text label. Canopy does a targeted single-prop update (the AND-8 scalar fast path); RN does a setState re-render. We measure tap-to-paint latency on each.

depth30.title: depth-30 layout
depth30.comment: 30 nested flex containers (each insetting 4dp) with a single text leaf — a pathological layout-depth stress for the flex/Yoga pass and the per-node create/mount cost.

driver.comment: toggle the whole subtree on/off `remounts` times to time cold mount + full teardown of a deep tree

gates.$comment: RND-9 will RATIFY these multipliers with owner sign-off; until then they are the PROPOSED bar from plans/10-competitor-master-plan.md (the M5 milestone). compare-report.js enforces them when given BOTH a canopy and an rn metrics file. They are multipliers of the RN reference on the SAME device — never absolute ms.
